summary.intent: Address five Codex review thread findings on PR #727 (checkbox adapters for ars-ui), push the fix commit, and bring CI to a green terminal state including Codecov patch coverage.
summary.outcome: All five Codex threads were replied to and resolved with commit b44ab88f. CI passed all lanes except Codecov patch coverage (76.83% vs 80% target), which was addressed by adding targeted unit tests for previously uncovered branches in xtask adapter scaffold and sketch validator logic. The coverage fix was committed and pushed.

repo: Codecov patch coverage gate is set at 80%; project-level coverage can stay green while a PR fails on patch coverage alone.
The repo uses a `@codex review` comment trigger to retrigger the Codex review bot after new commits; this is a deterministic step in the review workflow.
Adapter parity check (`cargo xtask lint adapter-parity`) scans directory-backed component modules recursively, not just mod.rs — this was one of the five Codex findings fixed in b44ab88f.
The xtask crate contains scaffold tooling (adapter.rs) and a spec/sketch validator (spec/sketch.rs) with error/no-adapter/no-form-control branches that were not covered by tests.

CI lanes run in a staged order: formatting and snapshots finish first, then clippy, then DOM browser/feature-core/adapter/unit tests, with Coverage and Release Verification being the slowest final lanes.
Commit hooks are present and can take 2+ minutes to complete silently for small patches.

code.finding: Uncovered branches include docs-only scaffolding path, non-form-control template selection, and the overwrite-refusal guard. New tests added to cover these.
Validation failure branches for missing sections, missing columns, and incomplete row cells were uncovered. A focused test was added for these paths.
E2E visual assertions were enhanced in b44ab88f to parse positive dimensions and assert checked/indeterminate/disabled/invalid computed-style deltas against an unchecked control baseline.

workflow: Running `cargo llvm-cov test -p xtask --text` with module-level filters provides annotated missed-branch output useful for targeting new tests at the patch coverage gap.
`cargo test -p xtask` with a single positional filter works; passing two positional test-name filters is rejected by Cargo.
Polling GitHub PR state (checks + reactions + unresolved thread count) in a 120-second sleep loop is the standard cadence for this review workflow.

When a slow CI job (Coverage) shows no terminal result after many polling cycles, fetching the job metadata directly via `gh api repos/.../actions/jobs/<id>` confirms whether it is genuinely in_progress vs. stuck.
Pre-commit hooks should be monitored with `ps` if they go silent for more than ~90 seconds before assuming they are stuck.

friction: Commit hook ran silently for ~2 minutes on a small patch, requiring a process-tree inspection to confirm it was still running.
Sandbox network wall blocked a `sleep 120 && gh ...` compound command mid-execution, requiring a manual re-run of the GitHub state check.
Codecov patch coverage failure was not caught until CI was nearly complete, requiring a second commit cycle after the main fix was already pushed.
Passing two positional test-name filters to `cargo test` silently failed (Cargo only accepts one); had to rerun with a single broader filter.

Codex's 👀 reaction persisted for the entire CI run without an intermediate signal, making it impossible to distinguish 'still reading' from 'waiting on CI' until threads appeared or the reaction changed.
open_items: Codecov patch coverage is borderline (the new tests bring it closer to 80% but the actual recalculated value was not confirmed in this session — CI result on the coverage-fix commit is still pending at session end).
The existing third-party future-incompatibility note in cargo xclippy output was acknowledged but not investigated or suppressed.
Codex had not yet posted a terminal review reaction (👍 or new threads) by session end; the review outcome is still open.
